title: Test Prep Strategies That Work (Without Cramming)
lead: Cramming might get you through the test, but the material won't stick. Here's how to prepare in a way that builds real understanding.

sections.title: Start Early
content: A week out is better than the night before. Spread review over several days. Even 15–20 minutes daily beats a 3-hour session the day before.

sections.title: Practice Under Test Conditions
content: Do practice problems with a timer. No notes if the test won't allow them. Simulating the real conditions reduces surprises and anxiety.

sections.title: Focus on Weak Spots
content: Don't waste time rehearsing what you already know. Use practice tests or self-quizzing to find gaps, then target those.

sections.title: Sleep and Fuel
content: A rested brain recalls better. A good breakfast helps. Cutting sleep to study usually hurts performance more than it helps.

cta.title: Prepare With Expert Help
cta.text: GuruForU tutors can create a targeted test prep plan for your child.

faq.question: When should my child start test prep?
faq.answer: A week out is better than the night before. Spread review over several days. Even 15–20 minutes daily beats a 3-hour cram session.

faq.question: How can practice under test conditions help?
faq.answer: Do practice problems with a timer, no notes if the test won't allow them. Simulating real conditions reduces surprises and anxiety.

faq.question: Should my child focus on weak spots or review everything?
faq.answer: Focus on gaps. Use practice tests or self-quizzing to find what you don't know, then target those areas. Don't waste time rehearsing what you already know.

faq.question: Does cutting sleep to study help?
faq.answer: No. A rested brain recalls better. Cutting sleep usually hurts performance more than it helps. A good breakfast on test day matters too.
